page: Vultr vc2-4c-8gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor. This configuration provides 4 shared vCPUs with 4 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 160 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate that this instance operates within the lower tiers of compute capability. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yielded scores of 1304.17 and 5030.23 respectively, placing them in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests showed an average cached read-only small block speed of 367.64 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read and write speeds dropped to 24.41 GB/sec and 19.55 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25%. Passmark CPU and memory scores further confirm these limitations, with the database operations benchmark scoring 637.68, which is in the bottom 10%.

Due to its shared CPU allocation, the vc2-4c-8gb exhibits lower performance stability under sustained compute loads, as demonstrated by its weak single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression results. However, the inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that do not require continuous high-performance compute. This server is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database testing where resource demands are bursty rather than sustained.
description: Vultr vc2-4c-8gb is a Cloud Compute virtual server featuring 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 160 GB local NVMe SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed, showing average cached read bandwidth of 367.64 GB/sec but weak uncached large-block transfer speeds. Given its shared CPU allocation and lower-tier benchmark results, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for non-critical workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, and small-scale testing where sustained high-performance compute is not required.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe storage for lightweight development and basic web hosting.

8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
